generatedSentence: During the annual festival, a special time of holidays in abeyance from normal life, young Elara felt a childish bliss. Her mental escape involved watching a feathered monkey climb a ladder whose diameter seemed proportional to the grand hothouse where exotic plants thrived. She felt a certain nostalgia for the jejune video games of her youth, though now her thoughts were more lissome. A preliminary attempt at origami felt unbaked, but she dreamt of a goblin’s nestbox. The rickey she drank was chalky, but she wouldn't amend her joyful spoken words. Her true adventure began when a rare violin was plucked from a museum filled with priceless objects. A notorious aristocrat, whose unique tattoo was a cutaway of the stolen object, openly defied all viability protocols. The detectives found scattered pistachios and nachos near the blackboard outlining the heist, suggesting a nerd with an odd sense of humor. This act of impiety was delivered with precise, intricate grace, like a perfectly folded origami. The villain had left his nickers as a taunt; the stolen item, still unpacked, had crossed vast oceans by sunrise, disappearing into the desolate djebels. They needed to repair this major breach. The detectives, led by a plumber who surprisingly possessed a knack for illicit operations, discovered the aristocrat’s limitation was an implicit paywall on the internet, his entire enterprise built on venal charity. He kept the stolen bejewels in a fortified vault, accessible via a secret gangway behind the embassy, blacked out from satellites. The jeweler, an assembler of intricate devices, was unwittingly feeding information to the vandal through looped signals. It was an unfunny joke, a masterclass in how gravity could be bent. They dished out milkshakes to calm their nerves while a seagull squawked, delivering sounds reminiscent of old westerns, providing a dash of hope to ever crack the greatest galactic divisions. Onboard a neon trolleybus, an eligible adjunct, resembling a flatter flake of chalky rock, provided the keynote speech for the operation. The mission: retrieve the violin from the hallows. Their investigation, buoyed by literature and irons, had yielded a clear verdict: the aristocrat was insolvent, funding his lifestyle by stealing from the railroads. A final passage through the city's underbelly, past dilapidated objects, revealed obtainable evidence. With the villain apprehended, the special instrument was returned, its beauty glowing with cinematic lighting. The victory brought a profound bliss, ending the adventure and setting right the scales of justice.
panelSentences: During the annual festival, a special time of holidays in abeyance from normal life, young Elara felt a childish bliss. Her mental escape involved watching a feathered monkey climb a ladder whose diameter seemed proportional to the grand hothouse where exotic plants thrived. She felt a certain nostalgia for the jejune video games of her youth, though now her thoughts were more lissome. A preliminary attempt at origami felt unbaked, but she dreamt of a goblin’s nestbox. The rickey she drank was chalky, but she wouldn't amend her joyful spoken words.

Her true adventure began when a rare violin was plucked from a museum filled with priceless objects. A notorious aristocrat, whose unique tattoo was a cutaway of the stolen object, openly defied all viability protocols. The detectives found scattered pistachios and nachos near the blackboard outlining the heist, suggesting a nerd with an odd sense of humor. This act of impiety was delivered with precise, intricate grace, like a perfectly folded origami. The villain had left his nickers as a taunt; the stolen item, still unpacked, had crossed vast oceans by sunrise, disappearing into the desolate djebels. They needed to repair this major breach.

The detectives, led by a plumber who surprisingly possessed a knack for illicit operations, discovered the aristocrat’s limitation was an implicit paywall on the internet, his entire enterprise built on venal charity. He kept the stolen bejewels in a fortified vault, accessible via a secret gangway behind the embassy, blacked out from satellites. The jeweler, an assembler of intricate devices, was unwittingly feeding information to the vandal through looped signals. It was an unfunny joke, a masterclass in how gravity could be bent. They dished out milkshakes to calm their nerves while a seagull squawked, delivering sounds reminiscent of old westerns, providing a dash of hope to ever crack the greatest galactic divisions.

Onboard a neon trolleybus, an eligible adjunct, resembling a flatter flake of chalky rock, provided the keynote speech for the operation. The mission: retrieve the violin from the hallows. Their investigation, buoyed by literature and irons, had yielded a clear verdict: the aristocrat was insolvent, funding his lifestyle by stealing from the railroads. A final passage through the city's underbelly, past dilapidated objects, revealed obtainable evidence. With the villain apprehended, the special instrument was returned, its beauty glowing with cinematic lighting. The victory brought a profound bliss, ending the adventure and setting right the scales of justice.
rawPanelSentences: During the annual festival, a special time of holidays in abeyance from normal life, young Elara felt a childish bliss. Her mental escape involved watching a feathered monkey climb a ladder whose diameter seemed proportional to the grand hothouse where exotic plants thrived. She felt a certain nostalgia for the jejune video games of her youth, though now her thoughts were more lissome. A preliminary attempt at origami felt unbaked, but she dreamt of a goblin’s nestbox. The rickey she drank was chalky, but she wouldn't amend her joyful spoken words.

Her true adventure began when a rare violin was plucked from a museum filled with priceless objects. A notorious aristocrat, whose unique tattoo was a cutaway of the stolen object, openly defied all viability protocols. The detectives found scattered pistachios and nachos near the blackboard outlining the heist, suggesting a nerd with an odd sense of humor. This act of impiety was delivered with precise, intricate grace, like a perfectly folded origami. The villain had left his nickers as a taunt; the stolen item, still unpacked, had crossed vast oceans by sunrise, disappearing into the desolate djebels. They needed to repair this major breach.

The detectives, led by a plumber who surprisingly possessed a knack for illicit operations, discovered the aristocrat’s limitation was an implicit paywall on the internet, his entire enterprise built on venal charity. He kept the stolen bejewels in a fortified vault, accessible via a secret gangway behind the embassy, blacked out from satellites. The jeweler, an assembler of intricate devices, was unwittingly feeding information to the vandal through looped signals. It was an unfunny joke, a masterclass in how gravity could be bent. They dished out milkshakes to calm their nerves while a seagull squawked, delivering sounds reminiscent of old westerns, providing a dash of hope to ever crack the greatest galactic divisions.

Onboard a neon trolleybus, an eligible adjunct, resembling a flatter flake of chalky rock, provided the keynote speech for the operation. The mission: retrieve the violin from the hallows. Their investigation, buoyed by literature and irons, had yielded a clear verdict: the aristocrat was insolvent, funding his lifestyle by stealing from the railroads. A final passage through the city's underbelly, past dilapidated objects, revealed obtainable evidence. With the villain apprehended, the special instrument was returned, its beauty glowing with cinematic lighting. The victory brought a profound bliss, ending the adventure and setting right the scales of justice.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A young woman with a single feathered earring and a shimmering, lissome silver jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara, a curious young woman, peering into a glowing hothouse filled with exotic plants and a tiny monkey climbing a ladder, bathed in early morning sunlight. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Detectives examining a cutaway diagram on a blackboard, next to scattered pistachios and nachos, while a silhouetted aristocrat with a distinctive tattoo vanishes into the djebels at sunrise, seen from a low-angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A plumber and a jeweler surrounded by complex wires and gears in a dimly lit, high-tech vault, while milkshakes sit on a table and a seagull squawks outside a window, depicted from a medium close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, now confident and resolute, standing onboard a neon trolleybus, holding the retrieved, glowing violin as the city skyline stretches behind her, shown from a dynamic wide shot.

definitions.definition: Expectancy; condition of ownership of real property being undetermined; lapse in succession of ownership of estate, or title.
definitions.example: The proceeds of the estate shall be held in abeyance in an escrow account until the minor reaches age twenty-one.

definitions.definition: Suspension; temporary suppression; dormant condition.
Expectancy of a title, its right in existence but its exercise suspended.
definitions.example: The broad pennant of a commodore first class has been in abeyance since 1958, together with the rank.

definitions.definition: To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.
definitions.example: The stylist feathered my hair.

definitions.definition: To rotate the oars while they are out of the water to reduce wind resistance.
To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.
definitions.example: After striking the bird, the pilot feathered the left, damaged engine’s propeller.

definitions.definition: To finely shave or bevel an edge.
To intergrade or blend the pixels of an image with those of a background or neighboring image.
To adorn, as with feathers; to fringe.
To render light as a feather; to give wings to.
To enrich; to exalt; to benefit.
To tread, as a cock.
To accidentally touch the cue ball with the tip of the cue when taking aim
Covered with feathers.
Having the blades of oars or propellers parallel to the direction of motion.
Having a finely bevelled edge.
Badly beaten.
(in combination) Having a specific type or number of feathers.
definitions.example: a blue-feathered bird

definitions.definition: Swift, like the flight of a feathered creature.
An event or community gathering, usually staged by a local community, which centers on some theme, sometimes on some unique aspect of the community.
In mythology, a set of celebrations in the honour of a god.
Fried cornbread
Pertaining to a feast or feast-day. (Now only as the noun used attributively.)
A reduction in consumer cost (usually for a limited time) for items or services rendered.
definitions.example: We're running a special on turkey for Thanksgiving.

definitions.definition: One of a rotation of meals systematically offered for a lower price at a restaurant.
definitions.example: Today's special is our tuna melt on rye.

definitions.definition: Unusual or exceptional episode of a series.
definitions.example: Did you see the Christmas special?

definitions.definition: A special constable.
Anything that is not according to normal practice, plan, or schedule, as an unscheduled run of transportation that is normally scheduled.
definitions.example: Thousands came to see the special that carried the President's coffin.

definitions.definition: Any unlicensed medicine produced or obtained for a specific individual patient.
A correspondent; a journalist sent to the scene of an event to report back.
A dispatch sent back by a special correspondent.
A light that illuminates a specific person or thing on the stage.
(nursing) To supervise a patient one-on-one.
Distinguished by a unique or unusual quality.
definitions.example: a special episode of a television series

definitions.definition: Of particular personal interest or value; dear; beloved.
definitions.example: Everyone is special to someone.

definitions.definition: Of or related to learning or intellectual disabilities.
definitions.example: He goes to a special school.

definitions.definition: Constituting or relating to a species.
definitions.example: The seven dark spots is a special property unique to Coccinella septempunctata.

definitions.definition: Chief in excellence.
Of or related to unconventional warfare, as in "special forces" and "special operations".
(now rare) Not nutritious.
(by extension, of a speech or an argument) Lacking matter; empty; devoid of substance.
Naive; simplistic.
A cocktail made with lime (citrus fruit) and carbonated water.
A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.
definitions.example: Today is a Wiccan holiday!

definitions.definition: A day declared free from work by the state or government.
A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).
definitions.example: I want to take a French course this summer holiday.

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.
definitions.example: a mortgage payment holiday

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
(usually after possessive) holiday, vacation; time off work, especially if spent travelling away from home
The season around Christmas, Hanukkah, and New Year's Day.
definitions.example: Where will you be spending the holidays?

definitions.definition: A heated greenhouse.
An environment in which growth or development is encouraged naturally or artificially; a hotbed.
A bagnio, or bathing house; a brothel.
A heated room for drying greenware.
A hot state in global climate.
(of a child) To provide with an enriched environment with the aim of stimulating academic development.
A proportion.
At a constant ratio (to). Two magnitudes (numbers) are said to be proportional if the second varies in a direct relation arithmetically to the first. Symbol: ∝.
In proportion (to), proportionate.
Of a typeface, having characters with natural (non-uniform) width (in contrast to monospace typefaces).
To preserve food (or sometimes other things) in a salt, sugar or vinegar solution.
definitions.example: These cucumbers pickle very well.

definitions.definition: To remove high-temperature scale and oxidation from metal with heated (often sulphuric) industrial acid.
definitions.example: The crew will pickle the fittings in the morning.

definitions.definition: (in the Python programming language) To serialize.
To pour brine over a person after flogging them, as a method of punishment.
To eat sparingly.
To pilfer.
An instance of processing something in brine or other solution.
A process by which the oxide scale on steel (especially stainless steels) is chemically removed (by strong acids).
Of or relating to the mind or an intellectual process.
Insane, mad, crazy.
definitions.example: He is the most mental freshman I've seen yet.

definitions.definition: Enjoyable or fun, especially in a frenetic way.
definitions.example: That was a mental party last night.

definitions.definition: Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
Any straight line between two points on the circumference of a circle that passes through the centre/center of the circle.
The length of such a line.
The maximum distance between any two points in a metric space
The maximum eccentricity over all vertices in a graph.
A frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened rungs (cross strips or rounds acting as steps).

definitions.definition: Flexible and graceful in movement; lithe.
Not baked or cooked.

To become better.
To heal (someone sick); to cure (a disease etc.).
To be healed, to be cured, to recover (from an illness).
To make a formal alteration (in legislation, a report, etc.) by adding, deleting, or rephrasing.

Resembling chalk in some way.

Sight; show; appearance; aspect.

A method of decorating the skin by inserting colored substances under the surface with a sharp instrument (usually a solenoid-driven needle).

definitions.example: Jones tattoos one into the gap in left; that will clear the bases.

definitions.definition: To remove from a package or container, particularly with respect to items that had previously been arranged closely and securely in a pack.
definitions.example: They didn't have time to unpack their bags before going out to dinner.

definitions.example: I worked all day and managed to get all the boxes unpacked.

definitions.definition: Not yet packed.
definitions.example: The unpacked gifts are on the table.

definitions.definition: (sometimes proscribed) Not yet having had its packing removed. (Especially after still. For more on this use, see http//itre.cis.upenn.edu/~myl/languagelog/archives/002171.html)
definitions.example: I left the box, still unpacked, at our old house.

definitions.definition: One of the aristocracy, nobility, or people of rank in a community; one of a ruling class; a noble (originally in Revolutionary France).
A proponent of aristocracy; an advocate of aristocratic government.
The encountering of risks; a bold undertaking, in which dangers are likely to be encountered, and the issue is staked upon unforeseen events; a daring feat.
A remarkable occurrence; a striking event.
definitions.example: A life full of adventures.

definitions.definition: A mercantile or speculative enterprise of hazard; a venture; a shipment by a merchant on his own account.

That which happens by chance; hazard; hap.

Risk; danger; peril.

To use a blackboard to assist in an informal discussion.
The state of being impious.
An impious act.
The lack of respect for a god or something sacred.

definitions.definition: An unattractive, socially awkward, annoying, undesirable, and/or boring, person; a dork.
definitions.example: Nerds seem to have fun with each other, but in a way that causes others to laugh at them.

definitions.definition: (post-1980s) A member of a subculture revolving around video games, fantasy and science fiction, comic books and assorted media.
The act of repairing something.
definitions.example: I took the car to the workshop for repair.

definitions.definition: The result of repairing something.
definitions.example: If you look closely you can see the repair in the paintwork.

definitions.definition: The condition of something, in respect of need for repair.
definitions.example: The car was overall in poor repair before the accident. But after the workshop had it for three weeks it was returned in excellent repair. But the other vehicle was beyond repair.

definitions.definition: To restore to good working order, fix, or improve damaged condition; to mend; to remedy.

definitions.definition: To pull something sharply; to pull something out

definitions.definition: To take or remove (someone) quickly from a particular place or situation.
To gently play a single string, e.g. on a guitar, violin etc.
definitions.example: Whereas a piano strikes the string, a harpsichord plucks it.

definitions.definition: To remove feathers from a bird.
To rob, fleece, steal forcibly

definitions.definition: To pull or twitch sharply.

definitions.definition: (university slang) To be rejected after failing an examination for a degree.

(of the strings of an instrument) Played by plucking.
Having courage and spirit; plucky.

A person employed to find information not otherwise available to the public.
A hill, a mountain (especially in the Middle East or North Africa).
To set free from restraint or danger.
definitions.example: deliver a captive from the prison

definitions.definition: (process) To do with birth.
To free from or disburden of anything.
To bring or transport something to its destination.
definitions.example: deliver a package;  deliver the mail

definitions.definition: To hand over or surrender (someone or something) to another.
definitions.example: deliver the thief to the police

definitions.definition: To produce what was expected or required.
To express in words or vocalizations, declare, utter, or vocalize.

definitions.definition: To give forth in action or exercise; to discharge.

definitions.definition: To discover; to show.
To admit; to allow to pass.
To administer a drug.
(in combination) That has been, or will be, delivered in a specific manner.

definitions.definition: The body of all written works.
The collected creative writing of a nation, people, group or culture.
(usually preceded by the) All the papers, treatises etc. published in academic journals on a particular subject.
Written fiction of a high standard.
definitions.example: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008

definitions.definition: The act of limiting or the state of being limited.
A restriction; a boundary, real or metaphorical, caused by some thing or some circumstance.
definitions.example: Getting into his wheelchair after his amputation, it felt like a limitation you could roll in.

definitions.definition: An imperfection or shortcoming that limits something's use or value.
A time period after which some legal action may no longer be brought.
definitions.example: The lawyer obtained impunity by dragging his obviously guilty client's case beyond the ten-year limitation.

definitions.definition: To decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
To pull with a jerk.

definitions.definition: To attach, tie or fasten.

definitions.definition: To marry oneself to; especially to get hitched.
Contraction of hitchhike, to thumb a ride.

A urologist.

To serve as, furnish with, or conduct oneself as though proceeding on a gangway.

The official residence of such a group, or of an ambassador.

To dip in dilute sulphuric acid; to pickle.
To vitriolize.

Anything resembling such a downward-facing concave structure, particularly the sky and caves.

Any cellar or underground storeroom.
Any burial chamber, particularly those underground.

definitions.definition: A piece of apparatus used for performing jumps.
A gymnastic movement performed on this apparatus.
An encrypted digital archive.

A person who needlessly destroys, defaces, or damages other people's property.

definitions.definition: One who assembles items.
A nanodevice capable of assembling nanodevices, possibly including copies of itself, according to a plan.
A Tex-Mex dish of strips of spicy marinated meat and/or vegetables in a soft flour tortilla, often served with salad or a savoury filling.
A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel.
A metallic chemical element having atomic number 26 and symbol Fe.
Any material, not a steel, predominantly made of elemental iron.

definitions.definition: A tool or appliance made of metal, which is heated and then used to transfer heat to something else; most often a thick piece of metal fitted with a handle and having a flat, roughly triangular bottom, which is heated and used to press wrinkles from clothing, and now usually containing an electrical heating apparatus.
(usually plural, irons) Shackles.
A firearm, either a long gun or a handgun.
A dark shade of the colour/color silver.
(shortened from iron hoof, rhyming with poof; countable) A male homosexual.
A golf club used for middle-distance shots.
Used as a symbol of great strength or toughness, or to signify a very strong or tough material.

definitions.definition: Weight used as resistance for the purpose of strength training.

definitions.definition: A safety curtain in a theatre.
Dumb bombs, those without guidance systems.
To pass an iron over (clothing or some other item made of cloth) in order to remove creases.
To shackle with irons; to fetter or handcuff.
To furnish or arm with iron.

definitions.definition: Shackles

definitions.definition: A lack of forward motion.
The stirrups.
A dry, thin, crispy baked bread (usually salty or savoury, but sometimes sweet, as in the case of graham crackers and animal crackers).
A short piece of twisted string tied to the end of a whip that creates the distinctive sound when the whip is thrown or cracked.
A firecracker.
A person or thing that cracks, or that cracks a thing (e.g. whip cracker; nutcracker).

A fine thing or person (crackerjack).

definitions.definition: An ambitious or hard-working person (i.e. someone who arises at the 'crack' of dawn).
One who cracks (i.e. overcomes) computer software or security restrictions.
A noisy boaster; a swaggering fellow.

A northern pintail, species of dabbling duck.
A pair of fluted rolls for grinding caoutchouc.
Crazy, insane.
A person whose job is making, repairing or selling jewelry.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.

definitions.definition: The transportation system comprising such roads and vehicles fitted to travel on the rails, usually with several vehicles connected together in a train.
A single, privately or publicly owned property comprising one or more such roads and usually associated assets

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.

definitions.definition: To transport via railroad.
To operate a railroad.

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.
To travel by railroad.
To engage in a hobby pertaining to railroads.
To manipulate and hasten a procedure, as of formal approval of a law or resolution.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.

To make holy, to sanctify.
A shout, cry; a hulloo.

A thin beverage, similar to the above, but with no ice cream or significantly less of it.

definitions.definition: An alkaline supplement administered to a horse to improve its racing performance.
The state or condition of having weight; weight; heaviness.
The state or condition of being grave; seriousness.

definitions.definition: The lowness of a note.
Force on Earth's surface, of the attraction by the Earth's masses, and the centrifugal pseudo-force caused by the Earth's rotation, resulting from gravitation.

Specific gravity.
The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
Any of the collections of many millions or billions of stars, galactic dust, black holes, etc. existing as independent and coherent systems, of which there are billions in the known universe.
Any print or pattern reminiscent of a galaxy, generally consisting of blending, semiopaque patches of vibrant color on a dark background.
Occurring at any time, occurring even but once during a timespan.
Always, frequently, forever.

At any time.

definitions.definition: As intensifier following an interrogative word.

A short run, flight.
A rushing or violent onset.
Violent strike; a whack.

definitions.definition: A dashboard.

A stand-in for a censored word, like "Devil" or "damn". (Compare deuce.)

definitions.definition: To throw violently.

Damn!

To make concave, or depress in the middle, like a dish.

Shaped like a dish; concave.

The loon (bird).

A pickpocket.
A competitor in certain sports who is known to regularly imitate being fouled, with the purpose of getting his/her opponent penalised.
To form something into a loop.
To fasten or encircle something with a loop.
To fly an aircraft in a loop.
To move something in a loop.

To duplicate the route of a pipeline.

Formed into a loop
(of part of a fingerprint) whorled
Drunk
Not funny, especially when attempting to be.

A petty contrivance; a toy.
Something performed, or to be done, requiring aptness and dexterity.
To crack; to make a sharp, abrupt noise; to chink.
To speak affectedly.

The symbol ̼ , which combines under a letter as a sort of accent.

To use a British Seagull outboard.
To work as a non-union casual stevedore.
A sensation perceived by the ear caused by the vibration of air or some other medium.

A distinctive style and sonority of a particular musician, orchestra etc
Noise without meaning; empty noise.
Earshot, distance within which a certain noise may be heard.

An instrument for probing or dilating; a sonde.

In general, an attitude of kindness and understanding towards others, now especially suggesting generosity.

definitions.definition: A feature of a website, application or service that only allows access to certain content or functions upon payment.
To restrict access to (a Web site or other resource) by means of a paywall.
Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.

definitions.definition: One who is eligible.
Allowed to and meeting the necessary conditions required to participate in or be chosen for something
Worthy of being chosen (for marriage)
Venous; pertaining to veins.

definitions.definition: An opinion or judgement.
definitions.example: a “not out” verdict from the umpire

Each of the separate parts of something resulting from division.
The process of dividing a number by another.
A calculation that involves this process.
definitions.example: I've got ten divisions to do for my homework.

definitions.definition: A formation, usually made up of two or three brigades.
A usually high-level section of a large company or conglomerate.
A rank below kingdom and above class, particularly used of plants or fungi, also (particularly of animals) called a phylum; a taxon at that rank.

definitions.definition: A disagreement; a difference of viewpoint between two sides of an argument.
A method by which a legislature is separated into groups in order to take a better estimate of vote than a voice vote.

To become a part of a group; to incorporate (someone) into a group.

Carried or used on or in a vehicle or vessel

definitions.definition: On or in a vehicle or vessel; aboard; on board.
Able to be obtained.
A bus, powered via overhead electric cables, that does not run on tracks
An appendage; something attached to something else in a subordinate capacity.
A person associated with another, usually in a subordinate position; a colleague.
An unmalted grain or grain product that supplements the main mash ingredient.
A quality or property of the body or mind, whether natural or acquired, such as colour in the body or judgement in the mind.
A key or scale closely related to another as principal; a relative or attendant key.

Having no variations in height.

definitions.definition: (voice) Without variations in pitch.
Having small or invisible breasts and/or buttocks.

definitions.definition: (note) Lowered by one semitone.
Of a note or voice, lower in pitch than it should be.

Uninteresting.

Lacking acidity without being sweet.
(of a battery) Unable to emit power; dead.

Lacking liveliness or action; depressed; dull and boring.

definitions.definition: Absolute; downright; peremptory.

definitions.definition: (of a golf club) Having a head at a very obtuse angle to the shaft.

definitions.definition: Fixed; unvarying.
